```
>
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> (ie comma+space is valid, space is valid, comma by itself is not).

Your prediction was wrong but thanks for trying anyway.

Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** "Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T08:55:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com>`

```

"Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote in message
news:bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com...
> Using netexpress 4.0 I get the following error
>
…[30 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Thanks for you help everybody.

The answer is to put parentheses around the 1st subscript.

 ((LAYT-PAPER),((WS-YMD-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD) )

Do we all agree its a bug?

regards

Steve
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T17:52:24+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<srRYa.2869$vo2.341@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com>`

```
Steve,
  I think you are CORRECT that this is a BUG in the Micro Focus product.
There is a (semi-)relevant rule in the 2002 COBOL Standard (where using
arithmetic expressions as subscripts were introduced).  It says,

"If the first operator in an arithmetic expression is a unary operator, it
shall be immediately preceded by a left parenthesis if that arithmetic
expression immediately follows an identifier or another arithmetic
expression.

    NOTE For example, when '1' and '+ 2' are used as subscripts for a
two-dimensional table A, the arithmetic expression '+ 2' needs to be
enclosed in parentheses, as in A (1 (+ 2))."

However, I do NOT think that this really impacts your (failing) example of:

    WS-STAT-LAYT-COUNT (LAYT-PAPER ((WS-YMD-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD) ).

which works when you code:

   WS-STAT-LAYT-COUNT ((LAYT-PAPER) ((WS-YMD-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD) )

*UNLESS* LAYT-PAPER is (itself) a table, you should NOT need to put
parenthesis around it as the  compiler SHOULD know that what follows is
another subscript for the original item, for for this item.

FYI,
  It is true that you MUST put a space after the comma between subscripts
(or put a space instead of a comma).  A comma is ONLY a separator when
immediately followed by a space.  However, you have indicated that is NOT
your current problem.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T14:59:42-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com>`

```
"Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote

> The answer is to put parentheses around the 1st subscript.
> 
>  ((LAYT-PAPER),((WS-YMD-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD) )

That forces (Layt-Paper) to be treated as an arithmetic expression and
prevents the parenthes around the 2nd expression making the parser
think that it is a subscript of Layt-Paper.

You are correct that the comma is a red-herring.  It is most likely
completely ignored and the compiler is acting as if it wasn't there.

   a.  (X 1)      is obviously 2 subscripts: X and 1
   b.  (X (1))    is one subscript: X which is subscripted by 1
   c.  ((X) (1))  is two subscripts: expressions (X) and (1)

> Do we all agree its a bug?
That is irrelevant, the questions are: 

   Does MF agree it is a bug ?

   How should the compiler disambiguate case b ?
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T22:10:34+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com>`

```
"Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com...
> "Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote
>
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
>    How should the compiler disambiguate case b ?


By the time that the compiler "gets" to case B, it should know whether X
requires a subscript or not.  If it requires one, then (1) must be a
subscript; if it does not require a subscript, then (1) must be another
subscript for the original table entry.   If the original table entry does
NOT have two-levels, then it is a source-code error.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T18:48:31-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0308081748.383e7b03@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com> <udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`

```
"William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com> wrote 

> >    b.  (X (1))    is one subscript: X which is subscripted by 1

> By the time that the compiler "gets" to case B, it should know whether X
> requires a subscript or not.  If it requires one, then (1) must be a
> subscript; if it does not require a subscript, then (1) must be another
> subscript for the original table entry.

Exactly.  The parser treated the expression as a subscript to
Layt-Paper and reported it as an error rather than moving on because a
subscript was invalid. This is a 'deficiency', or at least will be if
they claim 2002 conformance.

If the separator comma was actually being actioned instead of
discarded, then it may have guided the parser, but then it still has
to work with just a space separator which is also valid between a
variable and its subscript.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-08-23T22:21:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20030823182122.21183.00000460@mb-m10.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`

```
Bill Klien has commented 
(and I have heavily snipped)

>    b.  (X (1))    is one subscript: X 

>By the time that the compiler "gets" to case >B, it should know whether X
requires a subscript or not. 

Well, actually, the compiler does not get to B. It gets to the little pieces of
B one at a time and looks ahead trying to understand what the programmer has
coded. And that is a key note. Compilers need not only compile legitimate code
but they need to help us when we make an error. 

The full context of the original post, if I have grokked it all would be
something like the following:

TWO-DIM-ARRAT ( X (something-Y  

etcetera. Let me put a carat marker where any COBOL compiler writer is going to
have a real challenge:

TWO-DIM-ARRAY ( X ^ (something  

(the ^ carat marks a position in the parse forward scan).

When the parser has digested the two dimmensional array name, the first
parenthesis, and the data name X it is now looking ahead to the second open
parenthesis.

So there are atleast three possibilities. 

First, the second paren is the indication of a subscript of data item X. Bill's
assertion that the compiler 'should know' that X is not a subscripted item is
not exact enough for what compilers must do for us. We may make an error and
subscript X! And we need the compiler to tell us that is an error.

Second, and this has not been discussed. This is not meant to unnecessarily
confuse our valuable interaction. But the compiler writers deserve out fullest
support. The second paren could be the begining of a reference modification in
modern COBOLS. And that cannot be fully determined until it looks even
further-ahead and finds a colon.
And that is not necessarily right after the position of something-Y, if
something-Y is a parenthesized expression. In other words the lookahead
requirements here are not simple. Even if you wouldn't like to see a reference
modification here, a coder could do it, and the compiler has to be able to
parse through it successfully or get very lost.

Thirdly, the other possibility is the one the coder wants the compiler to
'see'.  The coder could be intending that the second paren is the begining of
an expression that has no relation to X. The problem here is that the coder
elected to write that expression as a parenthesized expression, which has
brought forth the ambiguity.

The more I look at this the more I move in the direction that there is no
compiler error here. If the second (or nth) subscript is a parenthesized
expression, you will have to parenthesize the preceeding expression (and that
recurses back to the next preceeding subscript, etc).

So, the notion that the compiler should know the type of the data item in
subscript postion 1, I humbly submit, is not correct for COBOL. And that is
because of the fact that we need the compiler to see those other things we
might do in the subscript area, intended or not, and legal or not, and diagnose
them.

Another possibility would be a mulformed function in subscript 1. The
malformation would be the absence of the word FUNCTION, but the presence of a
function name followed by parenthesized parameters.
(So a person might legitinately want max or min of some expression).

So, all in all, the original problem

TWO-DIM-ARRAY ( X  (something ))

(That is my abbreviated form).

might be said to look exactly like a reference to TWO-DIM-ARRAY with only one
subscript. And that subscipt is an invalidly subscripted variable X.  I think
the compiler was right.

Best Wishes to All
Bob Rayhawk 
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-24T03:27:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<%gW1b.2328$jY2.103@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net> <20030823182122.21183.00000460@mb-m10.aol.com>`

```
The 2002 Standard has explicitly CHANGED what is and is not valid as a
"subscript".  If you have the syntax


  Elem (A + (B (C)))

In a program to be compiled with a 2002 conforming compiler, there are LOTS
of things that this could mean and without knowing how many levels "Elem"
requires for subscripting and whether or not B requires subscripting, it is
impossible to parse this correctly (and/or tell if there is a compiler
error).

It is POSSIBLE that Elem requires 2 levels of subscripting and
  - level 1 is the value of A
  - Level 2 is the arithmetic expression "+ B (C)"

It is possible that Elem requires only 1 level of subscripting and
 - the subscript is the arithmetic expression "A + (B (C))"

It is possible that Elem requires NO subscripting or 3 levels of
subscripting and there is a source code error

It is possible that B doesn't require a single level of subscripting and
that there is a compile error

  ***

Again - with the CHANGE in the 2002 Standard allowing arithmetic expressions
as subscripts, it simply is IMPOSSIBLE to tell how to "parse" this phrase
without knowing how the various items  are defined and how many levels of
subscripts each item requires.

As far as the "requirement" for compiler to provide "user-friendly" messages
when there is a source code error, that is NICE when they can do it, but it
is not always possible for the compiler to make even a REASONABLE guess as
to which error you made.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-08-24T04:17:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20030824001743.06755.00001525@mb-m03.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<%gW1b.2328$jY2.103@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`

```
Yes, so far as what you posted, to wit,


>  Elem (A + (B (C)))

But what will be a problem is 


  Elem (A ( + (B (C))))

If we look ahead and find the plus we are not in difficulty. If we look ahead
and see a paren we are in difficulty. We need to cut the compiler writer some
slack.

In compiler parlance, the second open paren, just past the carat symbol here
below

  Elem (A  ^ (


must associate with A or we are nolonger talking COBOL.  It cannot dissociate
and become another expression, index of Elem or anything else.

Unless, of course we put parens around A, like so, ...

  Elem ((A  ^) (


The A can be reduced to an expression, and then,

  Elem ((A  )^ (


the next open paren is clearly another expression.

You can surround an expression with parens, but if you put that after a
dataname (without protecting the data name  with parens) then the compiler
needs to think you are subscripting, or reference modifying (or even malforming
a function invocation).

I maintain the compiler discussed was write, and the imposition of the parens
around the first subscript is correct (and the only viable solution). There is
no compiler error in the situation originally posted, IMHO.

Best Wishes,
Bob Rayhawk
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-24T04:56:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1AX1b.2829$jY2.1378@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<%gW1b.2328$jY2.103@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net> <20030824001743.06755.00001525@mb-m03.aol.com>`

```
Given:

 Elem (A ( + (B (C))))

If A requires 1 level of subscripting *and* Elem requires 1 level of
subscripting, then the rules of the 2002 Standard "clearly" state that "( +
(B (C)))" is a valid arithmetic expression and is a valid subscript for A -
and this occurrence of A is the subscript for B.

If, on the other hand, ELEM requires 2 levels of subscripting and A requires
NO subscripting then,

 A is the first subscript
    and
  (+ (B (C))) is the second subscript to identify which occurrence of ELEM
is being reference

If, on the other hand, ELEM requires 2 levels of subscripts *and* A requires
one level of subscript, then this is a source code error and there is NO WAY
for the compiler to tell which subscript is missing - the 2nd one for Elem
or the first one for A.

One may (or may not) LIKE it, but this is simply the way the 2002 Standard
is written.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-08-23T22:24:59-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0308232124.552a46@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net> <20030823182122.21183.00000460@mb-m10.aol.com>`

```
rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk) wrote

> Second, and this has not been discussed. This is not meant to unnecessarily
> confuse our valuable interaction. But the compiler writers deserve out fullest
> support. The second paren could be the begining of a reference modification in
> modern COBOLS. And that cannot be fully determined until it looks even
> further-ahead and finds a colon.

I don't think that a reference modified variable can be used as a valid subscript.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-24T19:41:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<gx82b.9102$8i2.7924@newsread2.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<udVYa.4451$vo2.4069@newsread1.news.atl.earthlink.net> <20030823182122.21183.00000460@mb-m10.aol.com> <217e491a.0308232124.552a46@posting.google.com>`

```
The word "function" becomes optional (in certain cases) in the 2002
Standard, so

    Numval  (field-name (reference-start : reference-length)

could be a subscript - but I agree that I don't think a reference modified
item (which is alphanumeric - or National or Boolean) could be a subscript
by itself.  (I have said this without checking all the rules.  I might be
mistaken on this.)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** "Hugh Candlin" <no@spam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T22:25:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<RrVYa.93297$0v4.6333173@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com>`

```

Richard <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com...
> "Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote
>
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
>    How should the compiler disambiguate case b ?

I asked how LAYT-PAPER was defined, but received no answer.

The answer to your question is simple.  There is no ambiguity.
The definition for LAYT-PAPER will direct
the compiler regarding any possible subscript requirements
when LAYT-PAPER is referenced.  In addition,
the definition of the elementary item dictates the need
for a multi-level subscript when it is referenced.

While I am always extremely cautious of assigning blame
to an OS or to any widely used software component,
I think it is a parse error in the compiler, absent clarification
of the actual definition of LAYT-PAPER itself.
```

_(thread depth: 4)_

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: malformed subscript

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Hugh Candlin" <no@spam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-09T02:42:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<gcZYa.90864$3o3.6287943@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<bgtv6d$7i3$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <bgvoi9$nc6$1@sparta.btinternet.com> <217e491a.0308081359.44fc7bb@posting.google.com> <RrVYa.93297$0v4.6333173@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net> <bh1c1r$9oc$1@sparta.btinternet.com>`

```

Steve Rainbird <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote in message
news:bh1c1r$9oc$1@sparta.btinternet.com...
>
> "Hugh Candlin" <no@spam.com> wrote in message
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > That forces (Layt-Paper) to be treated as an arithmetic expression
and
> > > prevents the parenthes around the 2nd expression making the parser
> > > think that it is a subscript of Layt-Paper.
…[40 more quoted lines elided]…
>           03 LAYT-STATUS                       PIC S9(2) COMP-3 .

Thanks.

There is no subscripting required for LAYT-PAPER,
so there is an error in the way the compiler parses.

Those PIC S9(2) COMP-3 should be changed
to PIC S9(3) COMP-3 by the way.  Some
might even suggest using a COMP filed instead.
```

#### ↳ Re: malformed subscript

- **From:** "ShadowFox" <me@email.com>

```
Steve I don't know net express but in any cobol I have ever used that first
inclusive parenthesis group is the subscript. It seems to include a
computational statement. If LAYT-PAPER is the subscript, and it is the
result of the computation((WS-YMF-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD)), try doing the
computation first. COMPUTE LAYT-PAPER =
((WS-YMF-MM * 100) + WS-YMD-DD)). Then just do a simple add to the
subscripted total. ADD 1 TO WS-STAT-LAYT-COUNT (LAYT-PAPER).
Hope this helped.
Bob
```

### Get the @rowcount from SQL to Power

#### ↳ Re: Get the @rowcount from SQL to Power

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-08-07T18:00:40-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0308071700.598bba5a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3213883.1060277215@dbforums.com>`

```
Carlos lages <member129@dbforums.com> wrote

> EXEC SQL BEGIN DECLARE  SECTION END-EXEC
> 01 sql-rowcount  pic s9(09)  comp-5
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> i GET COMPILER ERROR

I think that you are very confused.

My guess is that you are trying to obtain the number of rows in a SQL
SELECT from a PowerCobol form table attribute using some invented
non-syntax.

If the SQL resulted in a table on the form then the EXEC SQL is not
required - use that table's RowCount attribute in a MOVE statement. 
If you are trying to get this from an SQL Cursor then @RowCount can't
be used and the SET syntax is nonsense anyway.
```

```
Sql keeps  this information in @rowcount
I need to get  this .

there is nothing about  Power cobol table Objetc.

Carlos Lages
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Get the @rowcount from SQL to Power

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-08-09T08:48:38+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bh12df$td9$1@aklobs.kc.net.nz>`
- **References:** `<3213883.1060277215@dbforums.com> <3218103.1060358855@dbforums.com>`

```
Carlos lages wrote:

> I think you dont undstand me

I knew exactly what it is that you want, but you seemed to just make up 
what you thought may be code, but wasn't.
 
> If  i do that
> 
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> I want to know , how many rows  was selected.

Yes, I knew that.

> Sql keeps  this information in @rowcount

It may be that some specific versions of client interfaces for particular 
database systems maintain data items such as this. Which particular 
document did you read this in ?

For MS SQL Server (Sybase) the is an _extension_ that is 'SET ROWCOUNT ..' 
as you had but this is something _completely_ different:

"""SET ROWCOUNT limits the number of rows affected by all subsequent 
queries"""

This is not SQL, but is an extension.  Other databases do it with a LIMIT 
clause on the SELECT.  It is not the number of rows returned by a SELECT 
but is the maximum number that may be returned, if the select otherwise 
would return more then they are discarded.  There is also 'TOP n' in MS SQL 
(not ANSI) that is similar (but may result in different rows being 
returned).

> I need to get  this .
> How can i move @rowcount to a Cobol Host variable.

MS SQL Server (but not 'SQL') has a global variable @@rowcount. This is 
"The number of rows _affected_" and is not necessarily the number of rows 
found by a SELECT.  It _is_ the number of rows affected by a FETCH, UPDATE 
or INSERT.

It is probably what is returned in SQLERRD(3).
 
Remember - this is not 'MS SQL' that is running, it is Fujitsu client 
software, possibly using ODBC.

You could always do a 'pre select' of 

    SELECT COUNT(*) table WHERE ... INTO :myCount

or possibly write a database function to do it.

> there is nothing about  Power cobol table Objetc.

Power Cobol tables do define RowCount.
```

---

<a id="t-5e7e169f96"></a>
## Thread: Programming Project 4 — 6 msgs

### Programming Project 4

- **From:** "TerryMac" <tmaccaro@rochester.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T01:06:41+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<BICYa.168514$EQ5.155702@twister.nyroc.rr.com>`

```
Okay Guys!  Here is my project that I'm still trying to get it to work!

Input data from the file of book inventory records and store them in a
table.  You must check the book order records being input for the following
errors; range errors, type errors and reasonableness.  The data file is
sorted in ascending order by ISBN number.  You are also to verifty that the
input data from the file is in sequence (if this error occurs, print a
message and stop the program.) Invalid book order data will be output to an
error file and an entry will be made in an error report.  The program will
input from a screen a requst for a text book by ISBN or by Title.  You must
do a binary search if by ISbN.  You are to display the requested text as
shown in screen specifications #1( I've done the proper coding for this
don't worry about it).  You are to notify the user that the requested text
is not available as shown in screen specification #2 (I've done the coding
for this too - don't worry about it).  After displaying the search results,
ask the user if another search is desired, otherwise end the program.  Do
not include invalid data records in the table.
****************************************************************************
******************
Input: Master Book Inventory Input file will have the following format:

ISBN  - Required, must be 10 digits, in the format of 9-999-99999-9
including the hyphens
Publisher - Required field - 15 characters
Author - Required field - 15 characters
Title Required field - 25 characters
Edition - Not Required, Numeric (2 digits)
Copyright - Required field - four digit year, Reasonable test
Hardcover - Required field - (values Y or N)
Quantity on hand - Required field, numeric (up to 2000)
Number on backorder - Required field - numeric (up to 2000)
Estimate Delivery Date - Format YYYYMMDD, all zeroes if not on backorder
Cost - Required, numeric, 3 decimal places (up to 200.00)
Price - Required, numeric, 2 decimal places (up to 300.00)

Output: Your output files:  Error file (proj4.err); The error file is to
contain a copy of the record that was not included in the table.   Error
Report (proj4.rpt): The error report is to output the record not included in
the table and the reason(s) that the record was rejected
****************************************************************************
*******************
Here is the code I've done so far

 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. PROJECT-4.
       AUTHOR. THERESA MACCARONE
       DATE-WRITTEN. JULY 13, 2003.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
         SELECT INPUT-RECORD ASSIGN TO 'proj4.oms'.
         SELECT ERROR-RECORD ASSIGN TO 'proj4.err'.
         SELECT ERROR-REPORT ASSIGN TO 'proj4.rpt'.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD INPUT-RECORD
          LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD.
       01 BOOK-INVENTORY-REC.
          05 ISBN-IN                    PIC X(13).
          05 PUBLISHER-IN               PIC X(15).
          05 AUTHOR-IN                  PIC X(15).
          05 TITLE-IN                   PIC X(25).
          05 EDITION-IN                 PIC X(02).
          05 COPYRIGHT-IN               PIC 9(04).
          05 HARDCOVER-IN               PIC X(01).
             88 VALIDHARDCOVER VALUES 'Y', 'N'.
          05 QUANTITY-ON-HAND-IN        PIC 9(04).
          05 BACKORDER-IN               PIC 9(04).
          05 EST-DELIVERY-DATE-IN       PIC 9(08).
          05 COST-IN                    PIC 9(3)V9(3).
          05 PRICE-IN                   PIC 9(3)V9(2).
          05                            PIC X.
       FD ERROR-RECORD
          LABEL RECORDS ARE OMITTED.
          01 ERROR-RECORD-OUT           PIC X(93).
       FD ERROR-REPORT
          LABEL RECORDS ARE OMITTED.
          01 ERROR-REPORT-OUT           PIC X(132).
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 INVENTORY-TABLE.
          05 INVENTORY-ENTRIES OCCURS 25 TIMES
               ASCENDING KEY IS WS-ISBN INDEXED BY X1.
             10 WS-ISBN                    PIC X(13).
             10 WS-PUBLISHER               PIC X(15).
             10 WS-AUTHOR                  PIC X(15).
             10 WS-TITLE                   PIC X(25).
             10 WS-EDITION                 PIC X(02).
             10 WS-COPYRIGHT               PIC 9(04).
             10 WS-HARDCOVER               PIC X(01).
             10 WS-QUANTITY-ON-HAND        PIC 9(04).
             10 WS-BACKORDER               PIC 9(04).
             10 WS-EST-DELIVERY-DATE       PIC 9(08).
             10 WS-COST                    PIC 9(3)V9(3).
             10 WS-PRICE                   PIC 9(3)V9(2).
       01 WS-AREA-01.
          05 ARE-THERE-MORE-RECS PIC X VALUE 'Y'.
             88 NO-MORE-RECORDS    VALUE 'N'.
             88 MORE-RECORDS       VALUE 'Y'.
          05 NUM-CT                        PIC 99 VALUE ZEROS.
          05 MATCH-FOUND                   PIC X VALUE 'Y'.
          05 WS-ISBN-HOLD                  PIC X(13).
          05 FIRST-RECORD                  PIC X VALUE 'Y'.
          05 WS-DATE.
              10 WS-YEAR  PIC 9999.
              10 WS-MONTH PIC 99.
              10 WS-DAY   PIC 99.
       01 ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR PIC X VALUE 'N'.
       01 WS-COST-NUM PIC 999V999.
       01 WS-PRICE-NUM PIC 999V99.
       01 MENU-CODES.
          05 MENU-PICK PIC X.
             88 MENU-PICK-IS-VALID VALUES ARE 'I' 'T' 'Q'.
       01 ERROR-MESSAGE PIC X(79).
       01 SEARCH-CODES.
          05 BINARY-INDEX    PIC XBXXXBXXXXXBX.
          05 TITLE-INDEX     PIC X(25).
          05 X2              PIC 99 VALUE ZEROS.
       01 SEARCH-RESULTS.
          05 SEARCH-FOR-MESSAGE PIC X(25).
          05 SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE PIC X(5).
       01 SEARCH-AGAIN-INDICATOR PIC X.
       01 INVENTORY-RECORD-OUT-AREA.
          05 ISBN-OUT            PIC X(13).
          05 TITLE-OUT           PIC X(25).
          05 PUBLISHER-OUT       PIC X(15).
          05 AUTHOR-OUT          PIC X(15).
          05 EST-DELIVERY-DATE   PIC 9(08).
          05 QUANITY-ON-HAND-OUT PIC 9(04).
          05 COST-OUT            PIC 9(06).
          05 PRICE-OUT           PIC 9(05).
          05 BACKORDER-OUT       PIC 9(04).
       SCREEN SECTION.
       01 MAIN-MENU.
          05 BLANK SCREEN.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 1 VALUE 'THERESA MACCARONE'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'BOOK INVENTORY INQUIRY'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 64 VALUE 'DATE:' .
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 70 PIC 99 FROM WS-DAY.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 72 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 73 PIC 99 FROM WS-MONTH.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 75 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 76 PIC 9999 FROM WS-YEAR.
          05 LINE 3 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'SELECT SEARCH CRITERIA'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(I)SBN SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(T)ITLE SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 10 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(Q)UIT'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'YOUR SELECTION:'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 45 PIC X TO MENU-PICK.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 29 PIC X(79) FROM ERROR-MESSAGE.
       01 MAIN-MENU-02.
          05 BLANK SCREEN.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 1 VALUE 'THERESA MACCARONE'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'BOOK INVENTORY INQUIRY'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 64 VALUE 'DATE:' .
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 70 PIC 99 FROM WS-DAY.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 72 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 73 PIC 99 FROM WS-MONTH.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 75 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 76 PIC 9999 FROM WS-YEAR.
          05 LINE 3 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'SELECT SEARCH CRITERIA'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(I)SBN SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(T)ITLE SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 10 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(Q)UIT'.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 27 VALUE 'ENTER ISBN NUMBER TO SEARCH FOR'.
          05 LINE 15 COLUMN 28 PIC 9(10) TO BINARY-INDEX.
       01 MAIN-MENU-03.
          05 BLANK SCREEN.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 1 VALUE 'THERESA MACCARONE'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'BOOK INVENTORY INQUIRY'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 64 VALUE 'DATE:' .
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 70 PIC 99 FROM WS-DAY.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 72 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 73 PIC 99 FROM WS-MONTH.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 75 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 76 PIC 9999 FROM WS-YEAR.
          05 LINE 3 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'SELECT SEARCH CRITERIA'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(I)SBN SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(T)ITLE SEARCH'.
          05 LINE 10 COLUMN 29 VALUE '(Q)UIT'.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 27 VALUE 'ENTER TITLE TO SEARCH FOR'.
          05 LINE 15 COLUMN 28 PIC X(25) TO TITLE-INDEX.
       01 FILE-NOT-FOUND-SCREEN.
          05 BLANK SCREEN.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 1 VALUE 'THERESA MACCARONE'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'BOOK INVENTORY INQUIRY'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 64 VALUE 'DATE:' .
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 70 PIC 99 FROM WS-DAY.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 72 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 73 PIC 99 FROM WS-MONTH.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 75 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 76 PIC 9999 FROM WS-YEAR.
          05 LINE 2 COLUMN 33 VALUE 'SEARCH RESULTS'.
          05 LINE 4 COLUMN 33 VALUE 'SEARCH BY'.
          05 LINE 4 COLUMN 43 PIC XXXXX FROM SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 17 VALUE 'NO TEXT FOUND FROM SEARCH FOR'.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 47 PIC X(25) FROM SEARCH-FOR-MESSAGE.
          05 LINE 21 COLUMN 27 VALUE 'SEARCH FOR ANOTHER TEXT'.
          05 LINE 21 COLUMN 51 VALUE '(Y OR N)'.
          05 LINE 21 COLUMN 60 PIC X TO SEARCH-AGAIN-INDICATOR.
       01 FILE-FOUND-SCREEN.
          05 BLANK SCREEN.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 1 VALUE 'THERESA MACCARONE'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 29 VALUE 'BOOK INVENTORY INQUIRY'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 64 VALUE 'DATE:'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 70 PIC 99 FROM WS-DAY.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 72 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 73 PIC 99 FROM WS-MONTH.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 75 VALUE '/'.
          05 LINE 1 COLUMN 76 PIC 9999 FROM WS-YEAR.
          05 LINE 4 COLUMN 33 VALUE 'SEARCH BY'.
          05 LINE 4 COLUMN 43 PIC XXXXX FROM SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 8 VALUE 'ISBN'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 28 VALUE 'TITLE'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 49 VALUE 'PUBLISHER'.
          05 LINE 6 COLUMN 67 VALUE 'AUTHOR'.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 3 VALUE '--------------'.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 19 VALUE '-------------------------'.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 46 VALUE '---------------'.
          05 LINE 7 COLUMN 63 VALUE '---------------'.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 3 PIC X(10) FROM ISBN-OUT.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 19 PIC X(25) FROM TITLE-OUT.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 46 PIC X(15) FROM PUBLISHER-OUT.
          05 LINE 8 COLUMN 63 PIC X(15) FROM AUTHOR-OUT.
          05 LINE 11 COLUMN 3 VALUE 'QUANITY ON HAND'.
          05 LINE 11 COLUMN 25 VALUE 'COST'.
          05 LINE 11 COLUMN 35 VALUE 'PRICE'.
          05 LINE 11 COLUMN 45 VALUE 'QUANTITY ON BACKORDER'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 3 VALUE '----------------'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 23 VALUE '--------'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 34 VALUE '-------'.
          05 LINE 12 COLUMN 45 VALUE '---------------------'.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 19 PIC Z,ZZZ FROM QUANITY-ON-HAND-OUT.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 23 PIC $ZZZ.999 FROM COST-OUT.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 34 PIC $ZZZ.99 FROM PRICE-OUT.
          05 LINE 13 COLUMN 53 PIC Z,ZZZ FROM BACKORDER-OUT.
          05 LINE 16 COLUMN 45 VALUE 'ESTIMATED DELIVERY DATE'.
          05 LINE 17 COLUMN 45 VALUE '-----------------------'.
          05 LINE 18 COLUMN 52 PIC 9999/99/99 FROM EST-DELIVERY-DATE.
          05 LINE 21 COLUMN 27 VALUE 'SEARCH FOR ANOTHER TEXT (Y OR N)'.
          05 LINE 21 COLUMN 60 PIC X TO SEARCH-AGAIN-INDICATOR.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       100-MAIN-MODULE.
           OPEN INPUT INPUT-RECORD
                OUTPUT ERROR-RECORD
                       ERROR-REPORT
           PERFORM 200-DATE-ACCEPT-RTN
           PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECS = 'N'
                READ INPUT-RECORD
                  AT END
                     MOVE 'N' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECS
                  NOT AT END
                     PERFORM 250-ISBN-ERROR-RTN
                     PERFORM 300-DATA-VALIDATION-RTN
                END-READ
           END-PERFORM
           PERFORM 425-MAIN-SCREEN
           CLOSE INPUT-RECORD
                 ERROR-RECORD
                 ERROR-REPORT
           STOP RUN.
       200-DATE-ACCEPT-RTN.
           MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO WS-DATE.
       250-ISBN-ERROR-RTN.
           IF FIRST-RECORD = 'Y'
                 MOVE ISBN-IN TO WS-ISBN-HOLD
                 MOVE 'N' TO FIRST-RECORD
           END-IF
           IF ISBN-IN < WS-ISBN-HOLD
                 WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                   AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                 STRING
                   BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                   ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   'ISBN OUT OF SEQUENCE'
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                 END-STRING
                 WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                 STOP RUN
            END-IF
            IF (ISBN-IN(1:1) IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
               (ISBN-IN(2:1) IS NOT = '-') OR
               (ISBN-IN(3:3) IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
               (ISBN-IN(6:1) IS NOT = '-') OR
               (ISBN-IN(7:5) IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
               (ISBN-IN(12:1)IS NOT = '-') OR
               (ISBN-IN(13:1)IS NOT NUMERIC)
               WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                   AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                STRING
                   BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                   ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   'Invalid ISBN - Enter as n-nnn-nnnnn-n'
                   DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                END-STRING
                WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
             END-IF.
       300-DATA-VALIDATION-RTN.
           IF PUBLISHER-IN = SPACES
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'PUBLISHER DATA MISSING OR WRONG TYPE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF AUTHOR-IN = SPACES
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'AUTHOR FIELD DATA MISSING OR WRONG TYPE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF TITLE-IN = SPACES
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'TITLE FIELD DATA MISSING OR WRONG TYPE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
            END-IF
            IF (EDITION-IN IS NUMERIC) OR
               (EDITION-IN = SPACES)
                      CONTINUE
                    ELSE
                      WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'EDITION DATE FIELD IS WRONG TYPE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF (COPYRIGHT-IN IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
              (COPYRIGHT-IN IS NOT >= 1920)
                    WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'COPYRIGHT FIELD DATA INCORRECT OR MISSING'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF NOT VALIDHARDCOVER
                    WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'HARDCOVER FIELD DATA IS WRONG TYPE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF (QUANTITY-ON-HAND-IN IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
              (QUANTITY-ON-HAND-IN IS NOT >= 0) OR
              (QUANTITY-ON-HAND-IN IS NOT <= 2000)
                    WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'QUANTITY-ON-HAND FIELD DATA MISSING'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF (BACKORDER-IN IS NOT NUMERIC) OR
              (BACKORDER-IN IS NOT >= 0 AND <= 2000)
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'BACKORDER FIELD DATA IS WRONG'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF EST-DELIVERY-DATE-IN IS NOT NUMERIC
                      WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'ESTIMATE DELIVER DATE DATA IS WRONG'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           END-IF
           IF COST-IN IS NOT NUMERIC
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'COST FIELD DATA IS MISSING OR INCORRECT'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
            ELSE
                      MOVE COST-IN TO WS-COST-NUM
                        IF (WS-COST-NUM IS NOT >= 0.000) OR
                        (WS-COST-NUM IS NOT <= 200.000)
                          WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                               AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                                 STRING
                                   BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                                   ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                                   'COST FIELD DATA IS OUT OF RANGE'
                                   DELIMITED BY SIZE
                                   INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                                 END-STRING
                          WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                          MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT
                          ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                          MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
                        END-IF
           END-IF
           IF PRICE-IN IS NOT NUMERIC
                   WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      STRING
                        BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                        ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        'DATA IN PRICE FIELD IS MISSING OR INCORRECT'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                        INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      END-STRING
                      WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                      MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                      MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
           ELSE
                      MOVE PRICE-IN TO WS-PRICE-NUM
                      IF (WS-PRICE-NUM IS NOT >= 0.00) OR
                         (WS-PRICE-NUM IS NOT <= 300.00)
                         WRITE ERROR-RECORD-OUT FROM BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                            AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                         STRING
                            BOOK-INVENTORY-REC
                            ' ' DELIMITED BY SIZE
                            'COST FIELD DATA IS OUT OF RANGE'
                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
                            INTO ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                         END-STRING
                         WRITE ERROR-REPORT-OUT AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINE
                         MOVE SPACES TO ERROR-RECORD-OUT
                         ERROR-REPORT-OUT
                         MOVE 'Y' TO ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR
                      END-IF
           END-IF
           IF ERROR-FIELD-INDICATOR = 'N'
                PERFORM 400-LOAD-TABLE-RTN
           END-IF.
       400-LOAD-TABLE-RTN.
           PERFORM VARYING X1 FROM 1 BY 1
                      UNTIL X1 > 25 OR ARE-THERE-MORE-RECS = 'N'
                 READ INPUT-RECORD
                    AT END
                      MOVE 'N' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECS
                    NOT AT END
                   MOVE BOOK-INVENTORY-REC TO INVENTORY-ENTRIES(X1)
           END-PERFORM.
       425-MAIN-SCREEN.
           PERFORM 450-GET-MENU-PICK
           PERFORM 475-DO-THE-PICK
                 UNTIL MENU-PICK = 'Q'.
       450-GET-MENU-PICK.
           PERFORM 500-DISPLAY-MENU-SCREEN
           PERFORM 525-ACCEPT-MENU-SCREEN
           PERFORM 550-ERROR-MENU-SCREEN
                 UNTIL MENU-PICK-IS-VALID.
       475-DO-THE-PICK.
           IF MENU-PICK = 'I'
               PERFORM 575-DISPLAY-SCREEN
           END-IF
           IF MENU-PICK = 'T'
               PERFORM 600-DISPLAY-SCREEN
           END-IF
           IF MENU-PICK = 'Q'
                EXIT
           END-IF.
       500-DISPLAY-MENU-SCREEN.
             DISPLAY MAIN-MENU.
       525-ACCEPT-MENU-SCREEN.
             ACCEPT MAIN-MENU
             MOVE SPACE TO ERROR-MESSAGE.
       550-ERROR-MENU-SCREEN.
             MOVE "INVALID SELECTION - PLEASE RE-TRY."
                     TO ERROR-MESSAGE
             PERFORM 500-DISPLAY-MENU-SCREEN
             PERFORM 525-ACCEPT-MENU-SCREEN.
       575-DISPLAY-SCREEN.
           DISPLAY MAIN-MENU-02
           ACCEPT MAIN-MENU-02
           INSPECT BINARY-INDEX REPLACING ALL SPACE BY '-'
           PERFORM 675-BINARY-SEARCH-RTN.
       600-DISPLAY-SCREEN.
           DISPLAY MAIN-MENU-03
           ACCEPT MAIN-MENU-03
           PERFORM 700-SERIAL-SEARCH-RTN.
       625-RECORD-FOUND-SCREEN.
           DISPLAY FILE-FOUND-SCREEN
           ACCEPT FILE-FOUND-SCREEN.
       675-BINARY-SEARCH-RTN.
           SEARCH ALL INVENTORY-ENTRIES
              AT END PERFORM 725-FILE-NOT-FOUND-RTN
                 WHEN WS-ISBN(X1) = BINARY-INDEX
                  PERFORM 750-FILE-FOUND-RTN
            END-SEARCH.
       700-SERIAL-SEARCH-RTN.
           SET X2 TO 1
           PERFORM UNTIL X1 > 25
           SEARCH INVENTORY-ENTRIES
              AT END PERFORM 725-FILE-NOT-FOUND-RTN
              WHEN WS-TITLE(X1) = TITLE-INDEX
                  PERFORM 750-FILE-FOUND-RTN
           END-SEARCH
           SET X1 UP BY 1
           END-PERFORM.
        725-FILE-NOT-FOUND-RTN.
            IF MENU-PICK = 'I'
               MOVE BINARY-INDEX TO SEARCH-FOR-MESSAGE
               MOVE 'ISBN' TO SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE
            ELSE
               MOVE TITLE-INDEX TO SEARCH-FOR-MESSAGE
               MOVE 'TITLE' TO SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE
            END-IF
            DISPLAY FILE-NOT-FOUND-SCREEN
            ACCEPT FILE-NOT-FOUND-SCREEN
            PERFORM 775-SEARCH-AGAIN-RTN.
        750-FILE-FOUND-RTN.
            MOVE WS-ISBN(X1) TO ISBN-OUT
            MOVE WS-TITLE(X1)TO TITLE-OUT
            MOVE WS-PUBLISHER(X1) TO PUBLISHER-OUT
            MOVE WS-AUTHOR(X1) TO AUTHOR-OUT
            MOVE WS-EST-DELIVERY-DATE(X1) TO EST-DELIVERY-DATE
            MOVE WS-QUANTITY-ON-HAND(X1) TO QUANITY-ON-HAND-OUT
            MOVE WS-COST(X1) TO COST-OUT
            MOVE WS-PRICE(X1) TO PRICE-OUT
            MOVE WS-BACKORDER(X1) TO BACKORDER-OUT
            PERFORM 625-RECORD-FOUND-SCREEN
            PERFORM 775-SEARCH-AGAIN-RTN.
        775-SEARCH-AGAIN-RTN.
            IF SEARCH-AGAIN-INDICATOR = 'Y'
                 PERFORM 425-MAIN-SCREEN
                 MOVE SPACES TO MENU-PICK
                 MOVE SPACES TO BINARY-INDEX TITLE-INDEX
                 MOVE SPACES TO SEARCH-BY-MESSAGE
                 MOVE SPACES TO SEARCH-FOR-MESSAGE
            END-IF
            IF SEARCH-AGAIN-INDICATOR = 'N'
               PERFORM 425-MAIN-SCREEN
            END-IF.
****************************************************************************
*******************
My search would not work
I know something is wrong with my error validation

Let me know and I'll e-mail you the data file.  You will need a text reader
such as Notepad to open it.

I will continue to work on it over the next few weeks.
```

```
next go around - leave your code as above but comment it out  with *>  so he can

University of Limerick which are written using M/F - I can't recall, but
something there might help you with your current SORT project :-

taught !

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Programming Project 4

- **From:** "Harley" <dennis.harleyNoSpam@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T15:03:28+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4ZOYa.92686$0v4.6298800@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<BICYa.168514$EQ5.155702@twister.nyroc.rr.com>`

```

"TerryMac" <tmaccaro@rochester.rr.com> wrote in message
news:BICYa.168514$EQ5.155702@twister.nyroc.rr.com...
| Okay Guys!  Here is my project that I'm still trying to get it to work!
|

Terry,

I started looking at the code, and I have these comments, so far:

You are using consistent data names which is VERY GOOD.

You have a problem, you are reading the input file in 2 places.
You are putting out multiple copies of the same error record. (not the
report).
You must have 25 entries in your table, (OCCURS DEPENDING ON is in the
example below.)

Here are some suggestions:
These are personal style issues, others may disagree, and their arguments
shouldn't be ignored.

1. Create an executive routine to control the validation routines.
i.e. perform validate-isbn
     perform validate-whatever.
a. Put each field validation in a separate paragraph.
(This gives you a checklist of the fields in the executive routine)
   1.. set an invalid record indicator when you find a field in error.
b. After ALL fields have been validated, based on the invalid record
indicator, either
   1. write the invalid record
   2. add the record to the table

2 tips.

1. It is not uncommon to use a high level qualifier to identify a group like
a record, or table.
You MAY want to use a suffix to identify the type of data.
(i.e. QTY = Quantity, CNT = Count, ID = Identification, NBR = almost the
same as ID, YYYY = 4 digit year, YYYYMMDD = date format, IND = Indicator,
AMT = currency amount.)

2. See the example table included.
I prefix tables with T- followed by an identifier for the table.
The Index is prefixed with an I- followed by the identifier for the table.
i.e. T-INV uses index I-INV
(This makes checking that the index matches the table easier.)
(If your instructor has a WS- hangup, use the WS- prefix also)

I have reworked your record layout, and table to show you what I mean.
NOTE: I changed the table to use OCCURS DEPENDING ON.
When you add an entry, add 1 to the occurs counter, set the index to the
occurs counter, and update your data. (The max should be tested, and if you
have no more room, you may want to stop the run.)
(The scope of the search is limited by the occurs counter.)

000000 FD INPUT-RECORD
          LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD.
       01 INV-BOOK-INVENTORY-REC.
          05 INV-ISBN-ID                    PIC X(13).
          05 INV-PUBLISHER-NAME             PIC X(15).
          05 INV-AUTHOR-NAME                PIC X(15).
          05 INV-BOOK-TITLE                 PIC X(25).
          05 INV-EDITION-NBR                PIC X(02).
          05 INV-COPYRIGHT-YYYY             PIC 9(04).
          05 INV-HARDCOVER-IND              PIC X(01).
             88 INV-VALID-HARDCOVER VALUES 'Y', 'N'.
          05 INV-ON-HAND-QTY                PIC 9(04).
          05 INV-BACKORDER-QTY              PIC 9(04).
          05 INV-EST-DELIVERY-YYYYMMDD      PIC 9(08).
          05 INV-COST-AMT                   PIC 9(3)V9(3).
          05 INV-PRICE-AMT                  PIC 9(3)V9(2).
          05                                PIC X.


       01 T-INV-INVENTORY-TABLE.

          05  T-INV-OCCURS               PIC 9(4)  BINARY   VALUE 0.
          05  T-INV-OCCURS-MAX     PIC 9(4)  BINARY   VALUE 200.
If you want to change the number of entries in the table, change the OCCURS
below, and keep the MAX in sync.
          05  T-INV-INVENTORY-ENTRIES     OCCURS 0 TO 200 TIMES
                                         DEPENDING ON  T-INV-OCCURS
                                         ASCENDING KEY IS T-INV-ISBN-ID
                                         INDEXED BY I-INV.
             10 T-INV-ISBN-ID                 PIC X(13).
             10 T-INV-PUBLISHER-NAME          PIC X(15).
             10 T-INV-AUTHOR-NAME             PIC X(15).
             10 T-INV-BOOK-TITLE              PIC X(25).
             10 T-INV-EDITION-NBR             PIC X(02).
             10 T-INV-COPYRIGHT-YYYY          PIC 9(04).
             10 T-INV-HARDCOVER-IND           PIC X(01).
             10 T-INV-ON-HAND-QTY             PIC 9(04).
             10 T-INV-BACKORDER-QTY           PIC 9(04).
             10 T-INV-EST-DELIVERY-YYYYMMDD   PIC 9(08).
             10 T-INV-COST-AMT                PIC 9(3)V9(3).
             10 T-INV-PRICE-AMT               PIC 9(3)V9(2).
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Programming Project 4

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T11:43:09-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Cl-dnf0qR-eATq6iU-KYvw@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<BICYa.168514$EQ5.155702@twister.nyroc.rr.com> <4ZOYa.92686$0v4.6298800@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```
Harley wrote:
> You have a problem, you are reading the input file in 2 places.
> You are putting out multiple copies of the same error record. (not the
> report).

Second that. Your life become immeasurably easier if you read a file (or
write a record) in only one physical location/paragraph, performing that
routine whenever you need an I/O operation.

With multiple READ/WRITES it's too easy to skip an input record or double an
output record (or vice versa).
```

<a id="t-9a228915e1"></a>
## Thread: Restructure this! — 18 this month / 41 total

### Restructure this!

- **From:** Frank Swarbrick<Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-08T14:45:25-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`

```
I almost mentioned the term GOTO in my subject but decided it might be too
off-putting.  Let me give you a bit of some code.  It's bit verbose, but it
was easier to use real code than to make something up!


870-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.
**** DO WE NEED TO UPDATE RECORD # 1?                        
     EVALUATE TRUE                                           
       WHEN WIRECOMM-DELETE-WIRE                             
         EVALUATE TRUE                                       
           WHEN WIREMNT-NEEDS-APPROVAL                       
             ADD -1              TO APRV-ADJ-OUT             
           WHEN WIREMNT-NOTICE-PRINTED                       
             CONTINUE                                        
           WHEN OTHER                                        
             ADD -1              TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT             
         END-EVALUATE                                        
       WHEN WS-NOW-APPROVED                                  
         ADD -1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT             
         ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT             
         IF WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC                         
                 AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR 
             PERFORM 0680-ACCOUNT-LOOKUP THRU 0680-EXIT      
             IF WIRECOMM-TXFR-BRANCH = 218                   
                 MOVE +00001             TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             ELSE                                            
                 MOVE WIRECOMM-TXFR-BANK TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             END-IF                                          
             ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-IN          
         END-IF                                      
       WHEN WIRECOMM-IMMED-NOTICE                    
                 AND NOT WS-FIRST-NOTICE-PRINTED     
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT     
       WHEN WS-FOREIGN-AMT-CHANGED                   
                 AND WIRECOMM-NEED-APPROVAL          
         ADD +1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT     
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT     
       WHEN OTHER                                    
         GO TO 0870-FINISHUP                         
     END-EVALUATE.                                   
     SKIP1                                           
     IF WIRECOMM-BRANCH-NBR = 218                    
         MOVE +00001             TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR
     ELSE                                               
         MOVE WIRECOMM-BANK-NBR  TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR   
     END-IF.                                            
     SKIP1                                              
     PERFORM 3200-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.                      
     IF ERROR-OCCURED                                   
         MOVE ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WIRB-MESSAGE-LINEO  
         GO TO 0500-SEND-MAPWIRB.                       
     SKIP1                                              
     IF WS-NOW-APPROVED                                 
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC               
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR
         PERFORM 0660-WRITE-INCOMING                    
            THRU 0660-CONTINUE                          
     END-IF.                                            
 0870-FINISHUP.                                         
     MOVE WIRECOMM-FUNC          TO WIRECOMM-MAP.       
     GO TO 0714-BROWSE.                                 

As you can see, there is an evil GOTO (in then WHEN OTHER).  I'm curious as
to how someone might restructure this to avoid the GOTO.  Of hand I can see
creating a flag called UPDATE-HDR and then doing the following:

MOVE 'Y' TO UPDATE-HDR
(prior to the evaluate)
  WHEN OTHER
     MOVE 'N' TO UPDATE-HDR

And after the evaluate put
IF UPDATE-HDR
   ...everything between END-EVALUATE and 0870-FINISHUP goes here
END-IF

I could also avoid a new flag by saying
IF ZERO = APRV-ADJ-OUT AND NTCE-ADJ-OUT AND NTCE-ADJ-IN
  CONTINUE
ELSE
   ...everything between END-EVALUATE and 0870-FINISHUP goes here
END-IF

But this seems excessively verbose...

And really, why should I do this just to avoid using a GOTO.  Can I really
justify this?  Well, no I can't, but perhaps you can.

Not to start yet another GOTO war, but I was just reviewing this code and I
couldn't help myself!  (And this doesn't even reference the second GOTO...)



--- 
Frank Swarbrick
Senior Developer/Analyst - Mainframe Applications Development
FirstBank Data Corporation
```

#### ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-09T00:02:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<%RWYa.5405$BC2.2463@newsread2.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`

```
Frank,
  Is this for your usual (as I recall it) COBOL on VSE?  If so, the answer
is <joke>

"Ask your vendor of choice WHEN they will be providing the EXIT PARAGRAPH
syntax from the 2002 COBOL Standard"

If this is for Micro Focus or a few other compilers, then I would tell you
to use EXIT PARAGRAPH now.

Until "your" compiler supports it, the "GO TO ---exit" type logic is the
best (IMHO) approach - and probably even better than using a "flag".
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** Frank Swarbrick<Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-11T09:21:57-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bh8can$utr2p$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <%RWYa.5405$BC2.2463@newsread2.news.atl.earthlink.net>`

```
Actually, if you take another look at the code I posted you'll see that EXIT
PARAGRAPH, even if available, would not work.  My GO TO points to
0870-FINISHUP.  0870-FINISHUP does two things, neither of which is really an
EXIT.  Now, of course, the whole thing with the GO TO at the end of the
paragraph indicates that this paragraph is performed via a 'fallthru', which
is of course exactly what is happening.  Hey, I didn't write it originally! 
:-)

Frank

>>> William M. Klein<wmklein@nospam.netcom.com> 08/08/03 06:02PM >>>
Frank,
  Is this for your usual (as I recall it) COBOL on VSE?  If so, the answer
is <joke>

"Ask your vendor of choice WHEN they will be providing the EXIT PARAGRAPH
syntax from the 2002 COBOL Standard"

If this is for Micro Focus or a few other compilers, then I would tell you
to use EXIT PARAGRAPH now.

Until "your" compiler supports it, the "GO TO ---exit" type logic is the
best (IMHO) approach - and probably even better than using a "flag".
```

#### ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** "Harley" <dennis.harleyNoSpam@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2003-08-09T01:04:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<jMXYa.93451$0v4.6346051@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`

```
Frank,

Is this what you mean?

870-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.
**** DO WE NEED TO UPDATE RECORD # 1?
     EVALUATE TRUE
       WHEN WIRECOMM-DELETE-WIRE
            PERFORM   0871-WIRECOM-DELETE
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WS-NOW-APPROVED
            PERFORM   0872-WIRECOM-NOW-APPROVED
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WIRECOMM-IMMED-NOTICE
                 AND NOT WS-FIRST-NOTICE-PRINTED
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WS-FOREIGN-AMT-CHANGED
                 AND WIRECOMM-NEED-APPROVAL
         ADD +1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
     END-EVALUATE.

 0870-FINISHUP.

     MOVE WIRECOMM-FUNC          TO WIRECOMM-MAP.
     GO TO 0714-BROWSE.

 0871-WIRECOM-DELETE.

     EVALUATE TRUE
       WHEN WIREMNT-NEEDS-APPROVAL
         ADD -1              TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
       WHEN WIREMNT-NOTICE-PRINTED
         CONTINUE
       WHEN OTHER
         ADD -1              TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
     END-EVALUATE.

 0872-WIRECOM-NOW-APPROVED.

         ADD -1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
         ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
         IF WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC
                 AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR
             PERFORM 0680-ACCOUNT-LOOKUP THRU 0680-EXIT
             IF WIRECOMM-TXFR-BRANCH = 218
                 MOVE +00001             TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             ELSE
                 MOVE WIRECOMM-TXFR-BANK TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             END-IF
             ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-IN
         END-IF

 0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES.
     SKIP1
     IF WIRECOMM-BRANCH-NBR = 218
         MOVE +00001             TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR
     ELSE
         MOVE WIRECOMM-BANK-NBR  TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR
     END-IF.
     SKIP1
     PERFORM 3200-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.
     IF ERROR-OCCURED
         MOVE ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WIRB-MESSAGE-LINEO
         GO TO 0500-SEND-MAPWIRB.
     SKIP1
     IF WS-NOW-APPROVED
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR
         PERFORM 0660-WRITE-INCOMING
            THRU 0660-CONTINUE
     END-IF.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** Frank Swarbrick<Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com>
- **Date:** 2003-08-11T09:29:18-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bh8cog$utr2p$2@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <jMXYa.93451$0v4.6346051@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```
Hi Harley,

That looks fairly reasonable.  I'm still not especially fond of the multiple
coding of "PERFORM 0873-".  The GOTO just seems, well, cleaner and clearer. 
I'm iffy on the splitting of the 0871 and 0872 logic into their own
paragraphs, especially since you didn't do that for the code that has only
one or two lines.  But the more I look at it the more reasonable it looks. 
I'll have to ponder it...

Thanks,
Frank

>>> Harley<dennis.harleyNoSpam@worldnet.att.net> 08/08/03 07:04PM >>>
Frank,

Is this what you mean?

870-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.
**** DO WE NEED TO UPDATE RECORD # 1?
     EVALUATE TRUE
       WHEN WIRECOMM-DELETE-WIRE
            PERFORM   0871-WIRECOM-DELETE
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WS-NOW-APPROVED
            PERFORM   0872-WIRECOM-NOW-APPROVED
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WIRECOMM-IMMED-NOTICE
                 AND NOT WS-FIRST-NOTICE-PRINTED
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
       WHEN WS-FOREIGN-AMT-CHANGED
                 AND WIRECOMM-NEED-APPROVAL
         ADD +1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
         ADD -1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
            PERFORM   0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES
     END-EVALUATE.

 0870-FINISHUP.

     MOVE WIRECOMM-FUNC          TO WIRECOMM-MAP.
     GO TO 0714-BROWSE.

 0871-WIRECOM-DELETE.

     EVALUATE TRUE
       WHEN WIREMNT-NEEDS-APPROVAL
         ADD -1              TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
       WHEN WIREMNT-NOTICE-PRINTED
         CONTINUE
       WHEN OTHER
         ADD -1              TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
     END-EVALUATE.

 0872-WIRECOM-NOW-APPROVED.

         ADD -1                  TO APRV-ADJ-OUT
         ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-OUT
         IF WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC
                 AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR
             PERFORM 0680-ACCOUNT-LOOKUP THRU 0680-EXIT
             IF WIRECOMM-TXFR-BRANCH = 218
                 MOVE +00001             TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             ELSE
                 MOVE WIRECOMM-TXFR-BANK TO INCOMING-BANK-NBR
             END-IF
             ADD +1                  TO NTCE-ADJ-IN
         END-IF

 0873-WHATEVER-THAT-DOES.
     SKIP1
     IF WIRECOMM-BRANCH-NBR = 218
         MOVE +00001             TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR
     ELSE
         MOVE WIRECOMM-BANK-NBR  TO OUTGOING-BANK-NBR
     END-IF.
     SKIP1
     PERFORM 3200-UPDATE-WIREMNT-HDR.
     IF ERROR-OCCURED
         MOVE ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WIRB-MESSAGE-LINEO
         GO TO 0500-SEND-MAPWIRB.
     SKIP1
     IF WS-NOW-APPROVED
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR NUMERIC
             AND WIRECOMM-REF-NBR >= FB-TO-FB-1ST-REFNBR
         PERFORM 0660-WRITE-INCOMING
            THRU 0660-CONTINUE
     END-IF.

"Frank Swarbrick" <Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com> wrote in message
news:bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de...
| I almost mentioned the term GOTO in my subject but decided it might be
too
| off-putting.  Let me give you a bit of some code.  It's bit verbose, but
it
| was easier to use real code than to make something up!
|
…[58 more quoted lines elided]…
| As you can see, there is an evil GOTO (in then WHEN OTHER).  I'm curious
as
| to how someone might restructure this to avoid the GOTO.  Of hand I can
see
| creating a flag called UPDATE-HDR and then doing the following:
|
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
| And really, why should I do this just to avoid using a GOTO.  Can I
really
| justify this?  Well, no I can't, but perhaps you can.
|
| Not to start yet another GOTO war, but I was just reviewing this code and
I
| couldn't help myself!  (And this doesn't even reference the second
GOTO...)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2003-08-11T22:48:49-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bh9kih$cjh$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh8cog$utr2p$2@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh8e1f$i4c$1@panix1.panix.com> <bh94gn$vnd1g$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>`

```
In article <bh94gn$vnd1g$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>,
Frank Swarbrick <Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com> wrote:
><docdwarf@panix.com> 08/11/03 09:51AM >>>
>>In article <bh8cog$utr2p$2@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>, 
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>But I'd be a 'reasonable' monster, so that's OK, right?  :-)

Hmmmmm... I'm not sure which would be worse, a monster colpletely devoid 
of reason or a monster with sufficient reason to learn how to subvert the 
process of reasoning.

(Years ago I read yet another short story which had, as a subplot, a
computer which could weigh the relative merits of legal cases and render
judgements.  It cause me to ask, similarly, 'Which would be better... to
have one's fate decided by a jury, composed of humans, all too human... or
to have one's fate decided by a machine, which is anything *but* human?')

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net>
- **Date:** 2003-08-12T01:07:32-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<vjgthk5smknr16@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh8cog$utr2p$2@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh8e1f$i4c$1@panix1.panix.com> <bh94gn$vnd1g$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh9kih$cjh$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```

<docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:bh9kih$cjh$1@panix1.panix.com...
> In article <bh94gn$vnd1g$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de>,
> Frank Swarbrick <Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com> wrote:
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> >>146. He who fights with monsters should be careful lest he thereby
become
> >>a monster. And if thou gaze long into an abyss, the abyss will also gaze
> >>into thee.
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> to have one's fate decided by a machine, which is anything *but* human?')

I recall reading that Edison's first invention was a vote tabulating
device for use by legislatures. The machine would replace roll
call votes which were done alphabetically. The machine was
opposed by those whose names occurred later in the alphabet
because on close votes they controlled the outcome.

On the question of jury decisions, it would seem that a need to
control the outcome might affect one's choice. If the facts are on
one's side, choose the machine. Otherwise, choose to influence
the humans and, if necessary, argue the law.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2003-08-12T10:00:37-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bharu5$f2d$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<bh1257$tb83u$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh94gn$vnd1g$1@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de> <bh9kih$cjh$1@panix1.panix.com> <vjgthk5smknr16@corp.supernews.com>`

```
In article <vjgthk5smknr16@corp.supernews.com>,
Rick Smith <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote:
>
><docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:bh9kih$cjh$1@panix1.panix.com...
…[42 more quoted lines elided]…
>the humans and, if necessary, argue the law.

A lawyer once taught me 'If the facts are on your side then pound on the
facts.  If the law is on your side then pound on the law.  If neither is
on your side then pound on the table.'  

This indicates to me three emphases which could be used to sway a human
audience (factual, legal (matters of precedent) and emotional) towards a
given decision.  At least one of those (emotional) would be rather...
difficult to appeal to in a machine without some rather... intricate
programming.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Restructure this!

```

On 11-Aug-2003, "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote:

> I recall reading that Edison's first invention was a vote tabulating
> device for use by legislatures. The machine would replace roll
> call votes which were done alphabetically. The machine was
> opposed by those whose names occurred later in the alphabet
> because on close votes they controlled the outcome.

Funny.   It's like the stat about the "winning score" in sports.   The person
who scored run 4 in a 7-3 game wouldn't have been "winning" if there weren't
those 3 earlier runs that were just as important.

I read a SF novel where a legislature was in session.  They had a verbal vote. 
The voters listened and tried to hear which side won, but the computer tallied
the votes precisely.    All votes are as quick as the current "all in favor say
aye, all opposed nay" - or even quicker, as both responses could be
simultaneous, if desired.
```

```
"Frank Swarbrick" <Frank.Swarbrick@efirstbank.com> wrote in message
news:bh8cog$utr2p$2@ID-184804.news.uni-berlin.de...
| Hi Harley,
|
| That looks fairly reasonable.  I'm still not especially fond of the
multiple
| coding of "PERFORM 0873-".

You get a simple explicit structure that way.

| The GOTO just seems, well, cleaner and clearer.
| I'm iffy on the splitting of the 0871 and 0872 logic into their own
| paragraphs, especially since you didn't do that for the code that has only
| one or two lines.

That's because the size of the in-line 871, and 872 were making it harder to
see the structure of the outer EVALUATE, and it's a programmer's call on
what to do with the smaller procedures.

|But the more I look at it the more reasonable it looks.
| I'll have to ponder it...

You might want to code WHEN OTHER, CONTINUE to make your intentions more
explicit.

And you can remove the now obsolete paragraph 0870-FINISHUP.
I left it in to make it easier to see what I did.
```
